# Agile Risk Board Template

## Purpose
Provide a visual, collaborative approach to risk management that integrates with agile ceremonies and enables continuous risk awareness.

## Audience
Scrum Master, Product Owner, Delivery Team, Risk & Issue Manager.

---

## Document Information
- **Project/Product Name:**
- **Product Owner:**
- **Scrum Master:**
- **Team:**
- **Sprint/Iteration:**
- **Date:**
- **Version:**

---

## 1. Risk Board Overview
### 1.1 Key Principles
- **Visual management:** Risks are visible to the entire team.
- **Continuous monitoring:** Risks reviewed every sprint.
- **Collaborative ownership:** Everyone can raise and update risks.
- **Adaptive response:** Risk strategies evolve with learning.

### 1.2 Risk Board Benefits
- Faster identification and response to emerging risks.
- Stronger alignment with sprint goals and release objectives.
- Reduced escalation through early intervention.

---

## 2. Board Structure
### 2.1 Columns
```
IDENTIFIED  | IN PROGRESS | MITIGATED | CLOSED
```

### 2.2 Risk Card Format
```
RISK ID: R-###
TITLE: [Short description]
CATEGORY: [Technical/Business/Team/External/Process]
PROBABILITY: [Low/Medium/High]
IMPACT: [Low/Medium/High]
OWNER: [Name]
DUE: [Target date]
STATUS: [Identified/In Progress/Mitigated/Closed]
```

### 2.3 Color Coding
- **Red:** Critical (High probability + high impact)
- **Amber:** High/Medium risk
- **Green:** Low risk

---

## 3. Risk Identification & Assessment
### 3.1 Triggers
- Sprint planning
- Daily standups (blockers)
- Sprint reviews (stakeholder feedback)
- Retrospectives (process risks)
- Backlog refinement (story risks)

### 3.2 Agile Risk Scales
#### Probability
- **High (3):** Likely this sprint or next
- **Medium (2):** Possible this release
- **Low (1):** Unlikely but possible

#### Impact
- **High (3):** Sprint/release goal at risk
- **Medium (2):** Feature/story impact
- **Low (1):** Minor productivity impact

#### Priority Matrix
| Probability \ Impact | Low (1) | Medium (2) | High (3) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **High (3)** | Medium | High | Critical |
| **Medium (2)** | Low | Medium | High |
| **Low (1)** | Low | Low | Medium |

---

## 4. Risk Backlog Register
| Risk ID | Risk Description | Category | Probability | Impact | Priority | Owner | Status | Date Added |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |  |

---

## 5. Response Strategies
| Strategy | When to Use | Example |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Avoid | Eliminate risk source | Spike to validate approach early. |
| Mitigate | Reduce likelihood/impact | Add automated tests or buffer. |
| Accept | Risk within tolerance | Track in board and monitor. |
| Transfer | Shift ownership | Vendor escalation or specialist support. |

---

## 6. Integration with Agile Ceremonies
- **Sprint Planning:** Review risks that affect sprint goals.
- **Daily Standup:** Highlight changes to high-priority risks.
- **Sprint Review:** Capture stakeholder feedback risks.
- **Retro:** Review risk management effectiveness.

---

## 7. Metrics & Reporting
- **Risks per sprint**
- **Risk velocity (identified vs. resolved)**
- **Risk burndown**
- **Time to mitigation**

---

## Completion Checklist
- [ ] Risk board visible and updated weekly.
- [ ] Owners assigned to all high/critical risks.
- [ ] Mitigation actions tracked in sprint backlog.
- [ ] Risks reviewed in sprint ceremonies.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Team actively uses and updates the board.
- Critical risks have defined actions and owners.
- Risk trend reporting supports release planning.
